meta.title: 存在与虚无：哲学思辨
meta.description: Native-level philosophical discourse on existence and nothingness in Chinese: existentialism, absurdity, classical rhetorical patterns.
meta.description_ko: 원어민 수준의 존재와 허무 철학 담화: 실존주의, 부조리, 고전적 수사 문형.

explanation.en: 「存在主义」(cúnzài zhǔyì) means "existentialism", a philosophy emphasizing individual existence and freedom.
explanation.ko: 「存在主义」(cúnzài zhǔyì)는 개인의 실존과 자유를 강조하는 철학 사조를 뜻한다.

questions.prompt: 「存在主义」是什么意思？(What does 存在主义 mean?)

explanation.en: 「虚无」(xūwú) means "nothingness/void", a state devoid of substance or meaning.
explanation.ko: 「虚无」(xūwú)는 실체나 의미가 결여된 '공허함'을 뜻한다.

questions.prompt: 「虚无」是什么意思？(What does 虚无 mean?)
choices: 空无所有、缺乏意义(emptiness, devoid of meaning)

explanation.en: This existentialist phrase means one first exists, then defines their essence through their own choices and actions.
explanation.ko: 이 실존주의 명제는 인간이 먼저 존재하고 이후 자신의 선택으로 본질을 형성한다는 뜻이다.

questions.prompt: 「存在先于本质」是什么意思？(What does 存在先于本质 mean?)
choices: 人的本质在出生前就已注定
本质决定了存在的可能性
人首先存在于世，然后通过自己的选择塑造本质(one first exists, then defines one's essence through choices)

explanation.en: In philosophy, 「荒诞」(the absurd) refers to the conflict between humans' search for meaning and an indifferent, meaningless universe.
explanation.ko: 철학에서 「荒诞」는 인간의 의미 추구와 무의미한 세계 사이의 충돌을 뜻한다.

questions.prompt: 「荒诞」在哲学语境中是什么意思？(What does 荒诞 mean in a philosophical context?)
choices: 个体对意义的渴求与世界本身无意义之间的冲突(the conflict between the individual's craving for meaning and the world's inherent meaninglessness)

explanation.en: 「向死而生」means confronting death's inevitability to live more authentically in the present.
explanation.ko: 「向死而生」는 죽음의 필연성을 직시함으로써 현재의 삶을 더 진실하게 살아간다는 뜻이다.

questions.prompt: 「向死而生」是什么意思？(What does 向死而生 mean?)
choices: 正视死亡的必然性，从而更真切地把握当下的生命(confronting the inevitability of death in order to more authentically grasp present existence)

explanation.en: 「存在主义」means "existentialism".
explanation.ko: 「存在主义」는 '실존주의'를 뜻한다.

questions.prompt: 萨特是___的代表人物之一，强调个体的自由选择与责任。(Sartre is one of the representative figures of existentialism, emphasizing individual freedom of choice and responsibility.)

explanation.en: 「虚无」means "nothingness/void".
explanation.ko: 「虚无」는 '허무함'을 뜻한다.

questions.prompt: 面对宇宙的浩瀚无垠，个体有时会不禁感到一种深刻的___。(Facing the vastness of the universe, individuals sometimes can't help but feel a profound sense of nothingness.)

explanation.en: This describes the existentialist thesis that existence precedes essence.
explanation.ko: 이는 존재가 본질에 앞선다는 실존주의 명제를 설명한다.

questions.prompt: 这一哲学命题___，颠覆了传统上认为本质先于存在的观念。(This philosophical proposition — "existence precedes essence" — overturned the traditional notion that essence precedes existence.)
choices: 人的本质在出生前就已注定
本质决定了存在的可能性

explanation.en: 「荒诞」refers to the absurd condition.
explanation.ko: 「荒诞」는 부조리한 상황을 뜻한다.

questions.prompt: 加缪笔下的西西弗斯，正是___处境的经典象征。(Camus's Sisyphus is precisely the classic symbol of the absurd predicament.)

explanation.en: This describes Heidegger's concept of confronting death to live authentically.
explanation.ko: 이는 죽음을 직시함으로써 진실한 삶을 사는 하이데거의 개념을 설명한다.

questions.prompt: 海德格尔提出的___这一概念，鼓励人们不再逃避死亡，而是以此为契机深化对生命的领悟。(Heidegger's concept of "being-toward-death" encourages people to stop avoiding death and instead use it as an opportunity to deepen their understanding of life.)
choices: 正视死亡的必然性，从而更真切地把握当下的生命

explanation.en: 「凸显」means "to highlight/underscore".
explanation.ko: 「凸显」는 '두드러지게 하다'라는 뜻이다.

questions.prompt: 在浩瀚宇宙面前，个体存在的渺小___了人类对意义的执着追寻显得尤为珍贵。(In the face of the vast universe, the smallness of individual existence makes humanity's persistent pursuit of meaning all the more precious.)

explanation.en: 「与其〜不如〜」means "rather than 〜, it's better to 〜".
explanation.ko: 「与其〜不如〜」는 '~하느니 차라리 ~하는 게 낫다'는 뜻이다.

questions.prompt: 与其一味沉溺于对虚无的恐惧，___不如以积极的姿态创造属于自己的生命意义。(Rather than indulging in fear of nothingness, it's better to actively create one's own meaning in life.)

explanation.en: 「即使〜也〜」means "even if 〜, still 〜".
explanation.ko: 「即使〜也〜」는 '설령 ~일지라도 ~하다'는 뜻이다.

questions.prompt: 人生___充满了不确定性与荒诞，___依然可以通过自主选择赋予其独特的意义。(Even though life is full of uncertainty and absurdity, one can still endow it with unique meaning through autonomous choice.)

explanation.en: 「之所以〜是因为〜」emphasizes the reason.
explanation.ko: 「之所以〜是因为〜」는 이유를 강조한다.

questions.prompt: 存在主义哲学之所以在二十世纪引发广泛共鸣，___是因为它直面了现代人普遍面临的意义危机。(The reason existentialist philosophy resonated so widely in the 20th century is precisely because it directly confronted the crisis of meaning that modern people commonly faced.)

explanation.en: 「一旦〜就〜」means "once 〜, then 〜".
explanation.ko: 「一旦〜就〜」는 '일단 ~하면 곧 ~하다'는 뜻이다.

questions.prompt: 一个人一旦真正直面了存在的荒诞与虚无，___反而可能获得一种前所未有的自由与坦然。(Once a person truly confronts the absurdity and nothingness of existence, they might instead gain an unprecedented sense of freedom and equanimity.)

explanation.en: 「归根结底〜还是〜」emphasizes the fundamental point.
explanation.ko: 「归根结底〜还是〜」는 근본적인 요점을 강조한다.

questions.prompt: 归根结底，存在主义所探讨的核心问题___在于个体如何在无意义的世界中创造属于自己的意义。(In the final analysis, the core question explored by existentialism still lies in how the individual creates their own meaning in a meaningless world.)

explanation.en: 「共同」means "jointly/together".
explanation.ko: 「共同」는 '함께'라는 뜻이다.

questions.prompt: 由此可见，自由与责任___构成了存在主义伦理学的核心。(From this we can see that both freedom and responsibility jointly constitute the core of existentialist ethics.)

explanation.en: 「往往」means "often/frequently".
explanation.ko: 「往往」는 '흔히'라는 뜻이다.

questions.prompt: 众所周知，面对虚无与荒诞，个体___能够通过创造性的行动找到存在的价值。(As everyone knows, in the face of nothingness and absurdity, individuals often can find the value of existence through creative action.)

explanation.en: 「岂仅是〜」is a classical-style rhetorical construction meaning "how could it merely be 〜", emphasizing that something is far more significant than it appears.
explanation.ko: 「岂仅是〜」는 '어찌 단지 ~일 뿐이겠는가'라는 뜻의 고전적 반어 표현으로, 겉보기보다 훨씬 중요함을 강조한다.

questions.prompt: 这种对生命意义的追问，岂___偶然的哲学思辨，实乃人类共通的精神困境。(This questioning of the meaning of life is by no means merely coincidental philosophical speculation, but truly a spiritual predicament common to all humanity.)

explanation.en: 「始终」(shǐzhōng) means "always/all along".
explanation.ko: 「始终」(shǐzhōng)는 '시종일관, 늘'이라는 뜻이다.

questions.prompt: 面对生命的有限性，人类___在艺术、哲学与宗教中寻求超越有限存在的方式。(Facing the finitude of life, humanity has always sought ways to transcend finite existence through art, philosophy, and religion.)

explanation.en: 「不如」must come before the verb phrase 以积极的姿态〜: 不如以积极的姿态 (not 积极不如以的姿态).
explanation.ko: 「不如」는 동사구 '以积极的姿态〜' 앞에 와야 하므로 '不如以积极的姿态'가 맞다.

questions.prompt: 与其一味沉溺于对虚无的恐惧，积极不如以的姿态创造属于自己的生命意义。(Rather than indulging in fear of nothingness, it's better to actively create one's own meaning in life.) — find the error
choices: 与其一味沉溺于对虚无的恐惧，不如以积极的姿态创造属于自己的生命意义。
与其一味沉溺于对虚无的恐惧，积极不如以的姿态创造属于自己的生命意义。
与其一味沉溺于对虚无的恐惧，不如积极以的姿态创造属于自己的生命意义。
与其一味沉溺于对虚无的恐惧，以积极不如的姿态创造属于自己的生命意义。

explanation.en: The fixed pattern requires 是因为 in that order, not 因为是.
explanation.ko: 고정 문형은 '因为是'가 아니라 '是因为' 순서여야 한다.

questions.prompt: 存在主义哲学之所以在二十世纪引发广泛共鸣，因为是它直面了现代人普遍面临的意义危机。(The reason existentialist philosophy resonated so widely in the 20th century is precisely because it directly confronted the crisis of meaning that modern people commonly faced.) — find the error
choices: 存在主义哲学之所以在二十世纪引发广泛共鸣，是因为它直面了现代人普遍面临的意义危机。
存在主义哲学之所以在二十世纪引发广泛共鸣，因为是它直面了现代人普遍面临的意义危机。
存在主义哲学之所以在二十世纪引发广泛共鸣，是它因为直面了现代人普遍面临的意义危机。
存在主义哲学之所以在二十世纪引发广泛共鸣，它是因为直面了现代人普遍面临的意义危机。

explanation.en: 「一旦〜就〜」is the correct fixed pattern; 「才」does not match the immediate consequence implied by 一旦.
explanation.ko: 「一旦〜就〜」가 올바른 고정 문형이며 「才」는 '一旦'의 즉각적 결과와 맞지 않는다.

questions.prompt: 一个人一旦真正直面了存在的荒诞与虚无，反而才可能获得一种前所未有的自由与坦然。(Once a person truly confronts the absurdity and nothingness of existence, they might instead gain an unprecedented sense of freedom and equanimity.) — find the error
choices: 一个人一旦真正直面了存在的荒诞与虚无，就反而可能获得一种前所未有的自由与坦然。
一个人一旦真正直面了存在的荒诞与虚无，反而才可能获得一种前所未有的自由与坦然。
一个人一旦真正直面了存在的荒诞与虚无，才反而可能获得一种前所未有的自由与坦然。
一个人一旦真正直面了存在的荒诞与虚无，反而可能才获得一种前所未有的自由与坦然。

explanation.en: The sentence reads: 与其一味沉溺于对虚无的恐惧，不如以积极的姿态创造属于自己的生命意义 ("Rather than indulging in fear of nothingness, it's better to actively create one's own meaning in life").
explanation.ko: 문장은 '与其一味沉溺于对虚无的恐惧，不如以积极的姿态创造属于自己的生命意义' (허무에 대한 두려움에 빠져 있느니 차라리 적극적인 태도로 자신만의 삶의 의미를 창조하는 게 낫다)가 된다.

questions.prompt: 与其一味沉溺于对虚无的恐惧，__________创造属于自己的生命意义。(Rather than indulging in fear of nothingness, it's better to actively create one's own meaning in life.)

explanation.en: The sentence reads: 一个人一旦真正直面了存在的荒诞与虚无，就反而可能获得一种前所未有的自由与坦然 ("Once a person truly confronts the absurdity and nothingness of existence, they might instead gain an unprecedented sense of freedom and equanimity").
explanation.ko: 문장은 '一个人一旦真正直面了存在的荒诞与虚无，就反而可能获得一种前所未有的自由与坦然' (사람은 일단 존재의 부조리와 허무를 진정으로 직면하면 오히려 전례 없는 자유와 평온을 얻을 수도 있다)가 된다.

questions.prompt: 一个人一旦真正直面了存在的荒诞与虚无，__________可能获得一种前所未有的自由与坦然。(Once a person truly confronts the absurdity and nothingness of existence, they might instead gain an unprecedented sense of freedom and equanimity.)
